info.title: AlphaLens Briefs API
info.description: Read/write HTTP access to thematic briefs produced by the AlphaLens daily pipeline.

get.description:
``/v1/candidates/{date}/{ticker}`` — single candidate fetch.

The detail PK is supplied as a compound URL segment, not a single field.
The router pattern in ``urls.py`` is custom for this reason.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The initial index from which to return the results.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The initial index from which to return the results.

get.description: ``/v1/edge/outcomes`` — per-candidate rows (theme joined from the brief).

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: Calendar days back from the latest brief date (default: all).

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: Calendar days back from the latest brief date (default: all).

get.description: Returns whether the requested venue (defaulting to XNYS) holds a session on the anchor date, whether that session is a half-day, whether the venue is in a regular session right now, and the UTC ISO 8601 timestamps of the next session open and next session close. The day-level fields (``is_trading_day``, ``is_half_day``, ``next_open_iso``) are anchored on UTC today unless ``?as_of=YYYY-MM-DD`` is supplied; the intraday fields (``is_open_now``, ``next_close_iso``) always reflect the current wall-clock instant.

parameters.name: as_of
parameters.description: ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) to anchor the response on. Defaults to today (UTC). Date-only — time components are rejected with 400.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The initial index from which to return the results.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The initial index from which to return the results.

get.description: ``/v1/tickers/{ticker}/history`` — every brief appearance for one ticker.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The initial index from which to return the results.

Candidate.description:
One ranked candidate for a BULK list (day brief / theme / ticker history).

Auto-exposes every Brief field except the composite pk AND the heavy
``expert_assessments`` blob (see :class:`CandidateDetailSerializer`). The *_str
legacy denormalisations live only in DRF method fields, not on the model, so
they are not auto-exposed here.

CandidateDetail.description:
One candidate for the single-candidate DETAIL endpoint
(``/v1/candidates/{date}/{ticker}``) — identical to
:class:`CandidateSerializer` plus the full ``expert_assessments`` blob, fetched
on demand (e.g. for a card's deep-read drawer) rather than in every bulk list.

ChartBar.description:
One daily OHLC candle (folded from the cached minute bars, RTH-only).

``time`` is an ISO date string (``YYYY-MM-DD``) so it maps 1:1 to a
Lightweight-Charts daily bar time.

ChartMarker.description:
One modeled fill / exit marker, snapped to a daily bar ``time``.

``kind`` is the chart vocabulary (``ENTRY`` / ``TP`` / ``SL`` / ``TIME_STOP``);
``ambiguous`` carries the SL-first intrabar flag (a bar that touched both a TP
high and the SL low, resolved SL-first).

ChartPriceLines.description:
The resting ladder levels drawn as horizontal price lines.

``tp`` is the ordered list of take-profit targets. TIME_STOP is NOT a price
line — it is an exit event drawn only as a marker.

ChartResponse.description:
``/v1/edge/chart/<brief_date>/<ticker>`` — the pre-computed chart payload.

The SHAPE is stable across ``status``: a NO_DATA / NO_STRUCTURE payload carries
empty ``bars`` / ``markers`` and null/empty ``price_lines`` so the SPA branches
on ``status`` without a 2nd request. ``rth_only`` is always true in PR-1
(RTH-only daily candles; intraday is a later PR).

DayBrief.description: Full payload for one day: meta + every ranked candidate.

DayMeta.description: Row in ``/v1/days``: per-day metadata only (no candidates).

top_theme.description: Empty-string DB default → None on the wire (parity with legacy API).

DeploymentPanel.description: The DEPLOYMENT panel — N-INDEPENDENT, always populated.

EdgeOutcomeRow.description: The lean per-candidate shape for ``/v1/edge/outcomes`` (memo §5 table).

EdgePanel.description:
The EDGE panel — gated. ``status='insufficient'`` nulls the stat fields.

The key SHAPE is stable (every field always present); the N-gate hides the
numbers by setting them to ``null`` rather than dropping the keys, so the
frontend can build a single static type and branch on ``status``.

OpenPositions.description: Ongoing positions as a DESCRIPTIVE distribution (never a scalar mean).

PortfolioPanel.description: The PORTFOLIO (size-weighted) panel — gated, same N-gate as EDGE.

Stats.description: Top-line counters and most-frequent themes for ``/v1/stats``.

ThemeSummary.description: Row in ``/v1/themes``: distinct theme + appearance counts.

CloudflareAccessJWT.name: Cf-Access-Jwt-Assertion
CloudflareAccessJWT.description: Cloudflare Access service token / SSO JWT, injected by the Cloudflare edge after the user passes the configured identity provider (Google SSO). Verified server-side against the team's JWKS.
